canonical_question: What are the Salesforce Bulk API 2.0 capabilities, batch limits, and chunking requirements?
aliases: Salesforce Bulk API 2.0 limits and batch sizes
How does Salesforce Bulk API 2.0 handle large data loads?
Salesforce Bulk API 2.0 vs Bulk API 1.0 differences
What are the CSV format requirements for Salesforce Bulk API 2.0?

temporal_validity.last_breaking_change: v56.0 (Spring '23) — Bulk API 2.0 became default in Data Loader

constraints: Daily record limit: 100 million records per 24-hour rolling window across all Bulk API 2.0 jobs
Max upload size: 150 MB per job (base64 encoded); keep unencoded files under 100 MB
Concurrent jobs: max 25 ingest or query jobs running simultaneously (shared with Bulk API 1.0)
No serial processing mode — Bulk API 2.0 only supports parallel mode
Internal batch size: fixed at 10,000 records per internal batch — not configurable
Query result retrieval: results expire after 7 days; max 15 GB per query job
CSV and JSON only — XML format not supported (unlike Bulk API 1.0)

skip_this_unit_if.condition: User needs real-time individual record operations with <1s latency
User needs serial processing mode to avoid record lock contention
User needs event-driven integration with change notifications

related_to.label: Salesforce REST API capabilities for real-time individual record operations

alternative_to.label: Bulk API 1.0 — serial mode, XML support, manual batch control

often_confused_with.label: REST API is for real-time CRUD; Bulk API 2.0 is for async high-volume ETL

inputs_needed.question: What integration pattern do you need?
